title: Sykesia phanerophlebia
taxonomy: Sykesia phanerophlebia belongs to the family Lycaenidae and the subfamily Lycaeninae. It is a species of butterfly within the genus Sykesia. This genus is part of the tribe Cederini, which is a group of butterflies within the subfamily Lycaeninae. The classification of Sykesia phanerophlebia can be broken down as follows: Kingdom: Animalia, Phylum: Arthropoda, Class: Insecta, Order: Lepidoptera, Family: Lycaenidae, Subfamily: Lycaeninae, Tribe: Cederini, Genus: Sykesia, Species: S. phanerophlebia. The exact classification can vary depending on the source, but the above breakdown is a common one.

intro: Sykesia phanerophlebia, also known as the Himalayan Bells or the Red Bells, is a species of orchid native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce stress, improve respiratory health, and support cardiovascular well-being.
